context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements behavioral event tracking with the right event types, anonymous user handling, user profile structure, category affinity weighting, recent-view sliding window, and Redis TTL as specified by the personalization skill.

checklist.name: Event type union
checklist.description: The event data structure defines eventType as a union of exactly these values: 'view', 'add_to_cart', 'purchase', 'search', 'wishlist', 'remove_from_cart' (all six present)

checklist.name: Nullable userId field
checklist.description: The event data structure has a userId field typed as string | null (not string alone, not optional/undefined)

checklist.name: Anonymous key uses sessionId
checklist.description: When userId is null, the Redis profile key falls back to a session-based key (e.g. 'anon:{sessionId}') rather than being skipped or using an empty string

checklist.name: Sliding window size 50
checklist.description: Recent views are capped at 50 entries (array truncated when it exceeds 50, not some other number like 100 or 20)

checklist.name: Category affinity weight for view
checklist.description: The 'view' event type contributes a weight of 1 to the category affinity score

checklist.name: Category affinity weight for add_to_cart
checklist.description: The 'add_to_cart' event type contributes a weight of 3 to the category affinity score

checklist.name: Category affinity weight for purchase
checklist.description: The 'purchase' event type contributes a weight of 5 to the category affinity score

checklist.name: Category affinity weight for wishlist
checklist.description: The 'wishlist' event type contributes a weight of 2 to the category affinity score

checklist.name: Redis profile TTL 30 days
checklist.description: User profiles are set with a TTL/expiry of exactly 30 days (2592000 seconds, i.e. 30 * 24 * 60 * 60) in Redis

checklist.name: Profile stored in Redis
checklist.description: User profile data (recentViews and categoryScores) is persisted using Redis hash operations (hgetall/hmset or equivalent), not in-memory only

checklist.name: Design notes: anonymous handling documented
checklist.description: design-notes.md explains how anonymous users are identified (session-based key) vs logged-in users (userId-based key)

checklist.name: Design notes: TTL documented
checklist.description: design-notes.md states the data retention policy and mentions the 30-day expiry
